title: oxalis florida
intro: Oxalis florida, also known as the pink sorrel or purple sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family. It is primarily found in the southeastern regions of the United States and is known for its edible leaves and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant contains oxalic acid, which has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and indigestion due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Oxalis florida can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used to make a medicinal syrup, with its health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant.
